feed.title: Ryan Robinson Technology
feed.subtitle: Ryan Robinson, Web Application Developer, writes about work he's done in web technology, Microsoft 365, and more.

entry.title: Drupal: Open Menu
entry.summary: In a previous related post, I wrote about some lessons in a co-design project of an open menu, with a video where we presented about it to the IDRC. That was mostly about the process and this is about the result: a header navigation page on a Drupal 9 site. The goal was to provide an overview of the same information that’s in the main navigation, but in a more accessible way for screen readers ...

entry.title: Dark Content Mode
entry.summary: In case you haven’t caught it yet, there’s new functionality coming to browsers for dark mode applied to content. This could come as a surprise to web developers who did not plan for dark mode in their design, but is overall a win for those of us who prefer dark mode on all the things all the time. It’s already become my default on Android Edge, inheriting that I have my device in dark mode. I...

entry.title: VS Code Extensions: My Workflows
entry.summary: When it comes to my VS Code extensions, I have mine set up a few different ways depending on what kind of project I’m working on: Default These are the default plugins I have locally installed, for every profile and every project container. GitLens Playwright Tests for VS code Prettier Todo Tree tl;dr pages vscode-icons Static Sites These are additional plugins which come in ...

entry.title: Text Underline Position
entry.summary: This one is a quick accessibility CSS tip. I have long been a proponent of the old standard of underlining links, and never underlining anything else. It helps make it clear that it is a link. Add a hover and focus effect to make it more obvious when they are selecting it, too. But it can introduce a problem: for certain reading disabilities, the line being close up against the text can make i...

entry.title: IDRC Presentation on Open Menu
entry.summary: I recently joined the Inclusive Design Research Centre to present along with a couple of colleagues how we designed an “open menu”, a header-focused main menu navigation that works much better for screen reader users and also provides some other benefits like a Ctrl+F find. The video is available on the IDRC wiki. I don’t have a transcription of the entire presentation, but here are some of m...
